question: Is the meteorological alert text spelled exactly: "NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE RED ALERT: SEVERE HIGH-ALTITUDE CONVECTIVE STORM DETECTED, EVACUATE AIRSPACE IMMEDIATELY" (including punctuation)?
Is the meteorological alert text formed by thick cloud layers carved by wind (appearing as cloud-shaped lettering rather than a printed overlay)?
Is the flight code text spelled exactly: "UA1024 INTERNATIONAL FLIGHT ON FINAL APPROACH, CURRENT ALTITUDE 10,000 FEET, REQUESTING LANDING CLEARANCE"?
Is the flight code text formed by a white airplane contrail stretching across the sky?
Is the spectral analysis text spelled exactly: "SPECTROMETER READING: RED-ORANGE-YELLOW-GREEN-CYAN-BLUE-VIOLET FULL SPECTRUM SCAN COMPLETE" (including hyphens)?
Is the spectral analysis text embedded within the seven-colored arc of a rainbow with a glowing effect?
Is the electrical parameter text spelled exactly: "HIGH VOLTAGE SURGE DETECTED: POWER OUTPUT 1.21 GW, ENERGY CORE CRITICALLY OVERLOADED, DISCONNECT IMMEDIATELY"?
Is the electrical parameter text formed by lightning forks/brushstrokes at the edge of dark clouds?
Is the migration log text spelled exactly: "72ND AERIAL SQUADRON MIGRATION ROUTE BRAVO, ALL UNITS MAINTAIN FORMATION, HEADING SOUTH"?
Is the migration log text formed by a flock of migratory birds arranged in formation?
Is the time display text spelled exactly: "GREENWICH MEAN TIME: 3:15:42 PM, CALIBRATION IN PROGRESS"?
Is the time display text cast by sunbeams onto the cloud surface?
Is the navigation prompt text spelled exactly: "NAVIGATION SYSTEM ALERT: CUMULONIMBUS LAYER IN 3 MILES, REDUCE SPEED AND PROCEED WITH CAUTION"?
Is the navigation prompt text formed by low-altitude mist above a valley?
Is the status bar text spelled exactly: "ENVIRONMENTAL MONITORING SYSTEM: VISIBILITY EXTREMELY LOW, AUTOPILOT RADAR ENGAGED"?
Is the status bar text formed by raindrops gathering on a transparent "aerial glass"-like mist surface?
Are all generated text characters structurally complete, recognizable, and free from garbled symbols or merged strokes?
Is the overall image clear enough to read all text elements without excessive blur or artifacts?
Does the image adhere to a "dreamy sky illustration style" (concept art) rather than a photorealistic photograph?
